description: A designed single-subject 'DETAINED' card matching ICE Denver's standard template: medium-blue background with faint topographic-line texture. The centered square photograph is a booking/intake-style portrait of a woman with dark hair pulled back (some strands loose), looking at the camera in front of a white door/wall. A bold red banner across the bottom of the photo carries large white all-caps text 'DETAINED.' Below, large white serif type gives the name 'Blanca Ochoa Tello', followed by smaller white text 'WHEN OFFICIALS ARRESTED HER, THEY FOUND APPROXIMATELY 20,000 FENTANYL PILLS IN HER VEHICLE.' (the quantity/'fentanyl pills in her vehicle' bolded). Beneath that, spaced-out faint capitals read 'DENVER', and at the bottom is the DHS seal beside the white wordmark 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.' (Note: the accompanying tweet text discusses a different subject, Jacinto Gonzalez-Perez, and 'sanctuary cities'; the on-image content depicts Blanca Ochoa Tello and a fentanyl seizure.) The technique is a trophy/'busted' card tying an arrest to a large drug seizure to dramatize the public-safety threat. Target audience: general public and immigration-enforcement supporters.
notable_text: DETAINED; Blanca Ochoa Tello; WHEN OFFICIALS ARRESTED HER, THEY FOUND APPROXIMATELY 20,000 FENTANYL PILLS IN HER VEHICLE; DENVER
